title: Reflection should be avoided to increase accessibility of classes, methods, or fields
description: Reflection should not be used to increase accessibility of classes, methods, or fieldsAnalyze your codeadaptability-modularmaintainabilityCode SmellWhy is this an issue?More InfoAltering or bypassing the accessibility of classes, methods, or fields through reflection violates the encapsulation principle. This can break the internal contracts of the accessed target and lead to maintainability issues and runtime errors.This rule raises an issue when reflection is used to change the visibility of a class, method or field, and when it is used to directly update a field value.using System.Reflection; Type dynClass = Type.GetType("MyInternalClass"); // Noncompliant. Using BindingFlags.NonPublic will return non-public members BindingFlags bindingAttr = BindingFlags.NonPublic | BindingFlags.Static
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Identifies reflection calls that access non-public members which can break encapsulation
logic.triggering_conditions: Pattern matches reflection calls with BindingFlags.NonPublic or GetField/GetMethod/GetProperty calls that specify NonPublic access
exclusion_conditions: Simple method/property enumeration without access flags
never_trigger_on: Type metadata queries without member access
logic.reasoning: Detects reflection operations that access non-public (private, protected, internal) members of types, which can break encapsulation and make code harder to maintain. The pattern looks for BindingFlags.NonPublic or reflection methods that specify NonPublic access. Excludes legitimate uses like public reflection and type information queries.
